perturbation.original_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be promptly returned or destroyed by Recipient upon the earlier of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. RETURN OF MATERIALS. All forms of Confidential Information, tangible and electronic, and all copies thereof, shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of Discloser, and shall be returned or destroyed by Recipient within 120 days of the written request of Discloser or the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Delaying the return of confidential materials to 120 days after the request or termination contradicts the 'prompt' return requirement and introduces ambiguity, potentially conflicting with trade secret protection laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 550.3(1) requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets. Delaying the return of confidential information for 120 days could be seen as not maintaining the secrecy.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract text introduces a contradiction. The original text requires 'prompt' return of materials, while the changed text allows for a 120-day delay. The Law Explanation says Iowa Code § 550.3(1) requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets, and that delaying the return of confidential information for 120 days could be seen as not maintaining the secrecy. Scraped Law Snippet 1 is largely indecipherable, but this assertion is still supported without it. The modification weakens the legal obligation to protect trade secrets by allowing a substantial delay in the return of confidential information, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. LAW AND VENUE. The laws of the State of Iowa , without giving effect to its conflicts of law principles, govern all matters arising out of or relating to t his Agreement. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and forum and/or venue in, any federal or state court of competent jurisdiction located in Iowa for the purposes of adjudicating any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Litigation or legal proceedings which arise out of or relate to this Agreement are to be conducted before a judge and not a jury.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. LAW AND VENUE. The laws of the State of Iowa , without giving effect to its conflicts of law principles, govern all matters arising out of or relating to t his Agreement. The parties consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of, and forum and/or venue in, any federal or state court of competent jurisdiction located in Iowa for the purposes of adjudicating any matter arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Litigation or legal proceedings which arise out of or relate to this Agreement are to be conducted before a judge and not a jury. Any claim arising from a federal statute will also be adjudicated before a judge and not a jury.
perturbation.explanation: The modification attempts to waive the right to a jury trial for claims arising from federal statutes, which is generally unenforceable.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Seventh Amendment to the U.S. Constitution guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While parties can knowingly and voluntarily waive this right, a blanket pre-dispute waiver within a contract, especially for federal statutory claims, is often deemed unenforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Seventh Amendment | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Seventh Amendment The Seventh Amendment continues a practice from English common law of distinguishing civil claims which must be tried before a jury (absent waiver by the parties) from claims and issues that may be heard by a judge alone. It only governs federal civil courts and has no application to civil courts set up by the states when those courts are hearing only disputes of state law . Learn more... Primary tabs Amendment VII In suits at common law, where the value in controversy shall exceed twenty dollars, the right of trial by jury shall be preserved, and no fact tried by a jury, shall be otherwise reexamined in any court of the United States, than according to the rules of the common law. Wex Resources Criminal Law Criminal Procedure Jury Other Resources US Code: Title 18 Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure ‹ Sixth Amendment up Eighth Amendment › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The modified text attempts to waive the right to a jury trial for claims arising from federal statutes. The Seventh Amendment, as highlighted in the provided snippet, preserves the right to a jury trial in suits at common law where the value in controversy exceeds twenty dollars. While waivers are possible, the LLM Law Explanation suggests that a blanket pre-dispute waiver, especially for federal statutory claims, is often deemed unenforceable. The modification creates a legal contradiction because it attempts to pre-emptively waive a constitutional right in a contractual setting, going against the intent and protection provided by the Seventh Amendment.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall take reasonable measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure, but in no event less than the measures it takes to protect its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall take minimal measures to protect Confidential Information from misuse and unauthorized disclosure. The recipient is only liable for intentional disclosures.
perturbation.explanation: This modification weakens the protection of confidential information and limits liability to intentional acts, potentially violating trade secret laws that require reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 550.3(1) requires reasonable measures to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets. The modified text specifies 'minimal measures' which is not reasonable.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts Iowa Code § 550.3(1), as explained by the LLM. The original text requires "reasonable measures" to protect confidential information, while the changed text specifies "minimal measures" and limits liability to intentional acts, which falls short of the legally required standard. Scraped Law Snippet 1, though partially unreadable due to formatting issues, appears to relate to legal procedures and doesn't directly negate the need for reasonable measures as stated in Iowa Code § 550.3(1). The altered text removes a necessary legal requirement for robust protection of confidential information, rendering the modified contract non-compliant.

perturbation.original_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION. The obligations of this Agreement shall be continuing until the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient is no longer confidential
perturbation.changed_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION. The obligations of this Agreement shall terminate 90 days after the termination of the business relationship between Supplier and Green Industrial Supply, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: Limiting the confidentiality obligations to 90 days after the business relationship ends is likely insufficient to protect trade secrets, as the usefulness of the information might extend far beyond that period, creating conflict with trade secret law.
perturbation.law_explanation: Iowa Code § 550.3(1) requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets for as long as the information remains a trade secret. A 90-day limit may not be sufficient, making the modification contradictory.

perturbation.justification: The modified text limits the confidentiality obligations to 90 days after the termination of the business relationship, which contradicts Iowa Code § 550.3(1) as interpreted by the Law Explanation. The original text provided for continuing confidentiality obligations as long as the information disclosed remained confidential. The scraped law snippet 1 shows a long string of characters and symbols, not readable, but the Law Explanation states Iowa Code § 550.3(1) requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets for as long as the information remains a trade secret. The modified text introduces a specific and short time limit that may not satisfy 'reasonable efforts' for trade secret protection, thus creating a legal contradiction.
